perturbation.original_text: Terms of payment by the Agent to the Company for Products shipped to the Customer will be net thirty (30) days from the date of statement unless the Agent chooses to pay the Company via electronic funds transfer in which case the terms of payment will be net forty-five (45) days.
perturbation.changed_text: Terms of payment by the Agent to the Company for Products shipped to the Customer will be net sixty (60) days from the date of statement unless the Agent chooses to pay the Company via electronic funds transfer in which case the terms of payment will be net forty-five (45) days.
perturbation.explanation: The modification extends the standard payment term from 30 days to 60 days, while retaining the 45-day term for electronic funds transfers. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear which term applies if the agent does not choose electronic funds transfer, creating ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement. The contradiction specifically impacts the final sentence of section 4.3(b) and causes uncertainty on the due date of the payable ammounts upon termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'net thirty (30) days' payment terms, unless electronic funds transfer was used, then it was 'net forty-five (45) days.' The changed text alters the standard payment term to 'net sixty (60) days', while keeping the electronic funds transfer payment at 'net forty-five (45) days.' This creates a direct contradiction. Section 4.3(b) states that 'all amounts owed by either party to the other will become immediately due and payable as indicated in section 2.3.' Section 2.3 now has two payment terms dependent on the payment method, causing uncertainty on the due date of the payable amounts, particularly upon termination of the agreement as per section 4.3(b). This constitutes an inconsistency as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will compensate the Agent an additional two (2) percent through a discount off of the current price or promotional price of the Product times the total monthly units shipped at that price to the Customer excluding any taxes, and/or shipping and handling charges incurred by the Company, so as to compensate the Agent's sales representatives. Therefore, a total of ten (10) percent discount off of the current price list or promotional price times the total monthly units shipped at that price to the Customer will be applied as payment for services from the Agent including, but not limited to, the promotion, sales, and collection of payment from the Customer for Product shipped by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will compensate the Agent an additional two (2) percent through a discount off of the current price or promotional price of the Product times the total monthly units shipped at that price to the Customer excluding any taxes, and/or shipping and handling charges incurred by the Company, so as to compensate the Agent's sales representatives. Therefore, a total of twelve (12) percent discount off of the current price list or promotional price times the total monthly units shipped at that price to the Customer will be applied as payment for services from the Agent including, but not limited to, the promotion, sales, and collection of payment from the Customer for Product shipped by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The modification increases the total discount for the agent from 10% to 12%. This in-text contradiction directly conflicts with the pre-existing percentage stated earlier in paragraph 1.4(l), creating confusion and potential disputes over the correct discount rate to be applied. This creates an in-text contradiction within section 1.4(l) about the agent's earning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (l) follow the Company's credit policy (See Paragraph 2.3 Payment Terms) or program terms promptly in payment of goods shipped to the Customer. Product(s) shipped to the Customer will be billed to the Agent at the price stated on a current price list or according to the current price program minus a ten (10) percent discount as outlined in Paragraph 1.5(d). It is the responsibility of the Agent to evaluate the credit worthiness of the Customer. Upon transmitting an order for shipment to the Company, the Agent accepts full responsibility for payment of the product from the Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 1.4(l) specifies a 10% discount for the agent. The changed text in section 1.4(l) increases this discount to 12%. The contradicted text, also in paragraph 1.4(l), explicitly states that the Product(s) shipped to the Customer will be billed to the Agent at the price stated on a current price list or according to the current price program minus a ten (10) percent discount as outlined in Paragraph 1.5(d). The change from 10% to 12% directly contradicts the original 10% discount established in both the original version of the changed text and the contradicted text.
